phases.name: Year 1
phases.subtitle: Explore
phases.description: Mathematics, chemistry, and engineering fundamentals establish the foundation for bioprocess engineering.

milestones.label: Explore engineering career pathways
milestones.description: Introduction to engineering disciplines, problem-solving methods, and professional ethics. Students explore bioprocess engineering career pathways in biofuels, pharmaceuticals, and environmental remediation through industry panels and lab tours.

milestones.label: Master calculus I foundations
milestones.description: Limits, derivatives, and integrals with engineering applications. Essential mathematical foundation for all subsequent engineering coursework including thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, and process design.

milestones.label: Introduction to biological engineering
milestones.description: Survey of biological engineering principles including bioprocess design, biomaterials, and environmental engineering. Students complete hands-on projects integrating biology and engineering concepts.

milestones.label: Complete general chemistry
milestones.courseRef: CHEM 106/116 — General Chemistry I with Lab
milestones.description: Fundamental chemistry principles essential for understanding biochemical reactions, thermodynamics, and material properties in biological engineering systems.

phases.name: Year 2
phases.subtitle: Engage
phases.description: Advanced mathematics, physics, and core engineering sciences prepare students for specialized bioprocess coursework.

milestones.label: Complete calculus II & differential equations
milestones.description: Integration techniques, series, and introduction to differential equations. Mathematical tools for modeling bioprocess kinetics, mass transfer, and reactor design.

milestones.label: Master engineering physics
milestones.description: Mechanics, thermodynamics, and wave phenomena with engineering applications. Laboratory component develops experimental design and data analysis skills for engineering practice.

milestones.label: Study biology for engineers
milestones.description: Cell biology, molecular genetics, and microbiology fundamentals for biological engineers. Emphasis on biological systems that can be engineered for industrial, medical, and environmental applications.

milestones.label: Master engineering graphics & CAD
milestones.description: Technical drawing, CAD software, and 3D modeling for engineering design communication. Students produce process flow diagrams and equipment layouts used in bioprocess facility design.

phases.name: Year 3
phases.subtitle: Develop
phases.description: Bioprocess-specific coursework in bioreactor design, biomass conversion, and pharmaceutical engineering develops professional specialization.

milestones.label: Master bioprocess engineering fundamentals
milestones.description: Mass and energy balances for biological systems, bioreactor design, sterilization, and downstream processing. Core course for the bioprocess engineering track covering industrial fermentation and bioproduct recovery.

milestones.label: Study biomass conversion technologies
milestones.description: Biomass feedstocks, conversion pathways (biochemical, thermochemical), biofuel production, and biorefinery concepts. Students analyze global bioenergy markets and evaluate sustainability of different conversion technologies.

milestones.label: Complete transport phenomena for biosystems
milestones.description: Heat and mass transfer in biological systems, membrane separations, and bioseparation processes. Advanced engineering science applied to pharmaceutical purification and biological product recovery.

milestones.label: Study biochemical engineering
milestones.description: Enzyme kinetics, microbial growth models, bioreactor scale-up, and pharmaceutical bioprocess development. Students design fed-batch and continuous fermentation processes for real bioproducts.

phases.name: Year 4
phases.subtitle: Launch
phases.description: Industry-sponsored capstone, FE exam preparation, and professional placement complete ABET accreditation requirements.

milestones.label: Begin two-semester capstone design
milestones.description: First semester of industry-sponsored capstone design project. Student teams identify a bioprocess engineering challenge, conduct literature review, develop design criteria, and produce preliminary design proposals reviewed by faculty and industry mentors.

milestones.label: Complete capstone design & presentation
milestones.description: Final capstone design project delivery. Teams produce complete engineering designs with economic analysis, environmental impact assessment, and professional presentations to industry sponsors. Demonstrates ABET Criterion 5 design competency.

milestones.label: Prepare for FE exam certification
milestones.description: Structured preparation for the NCEES Fundamentals of Engineering exam. Review sessions cover mathematics, thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, and biological engineering topics aligned with FE exam specifications.

milestones.label: Complete professional engineering internship
milestones.description: Supervised engineering experience at a biopharmaceutical company, biofuel producer, environmental engineering firm, or government agency. Students apply ABET competencies in authentic professional settings and begin PE licensure pathway.
